description.content: Health checks and system information

Returns the liveness status of the API and its dependencies 
(PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO/R2). Used by load balancers and uptime monitors.

response.name: One or more dependencies are unhealthy

description.content: Registration, login, token refresh, email verification, password reset
Returns the authenticated user's profile and role.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Creates a new seeker or vendor account and sends an email verification link.
The account is not fully active until the email is verified.

response.name: Account created; verification email sent

description.content: Authenticates with email + password and returns access and refresh tokens.
Revokes the current refresh token. The access token expires naturally (TTL).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

Confirms the email address using the token sent in the verification email.
Tokens expire after 24 hours.
Sends a password-reset email if the address is registered.

response.name: Reset email sent (always 200 to prevent email enumeration)

description.content: Sets a new password using the token from the reset email. Tokens expire after 1 hour.

response.name: Password updated; all existing refresh tokens revoked

description.content:
Sends a one-time passcode via email (Resend) or SMS (Twilio Verify).
Rate limited to 5 requests per minute per IP.
Validates a one-time passcode for contact verification or MFA step-up.

Enables SMS or email OTP as a second factor for vendor/admin accounts.
Requires an authenticated session and a verified contact method.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Second step after password login when MFA is enabled. Returns full auth tokens on success.
Job posting, search, and management

Creates a new job posting. Behaviour differs by caller role:

- **admin**: may specify any `companyId`; status defaults to `published`
- **vendor**: `companyId` is inferred from the caller's company; must supply 
  `companyPackageId` to consume a posting credit; status defaults to `draft`

Screening questions (if provided) are created atomically with the job.

response.name: Job created

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Soft-deletes the job (status → closed). Only admins or the owning vendor.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Bulk job ingestion for scrapers and feed partners

Accepts a batch of job records from scrapers or feed partners.

**Sync mode** (≤100 jobs): processed inline; result returned immediately.
**Async mode** (>100 jobs): enqueued via BullMQ; returns a `batchId` to poll.

Supports two content types:
- `application/json` — JSON payload with a `jobs` array
- `multipart/form-data` — CSV file upload (one row per job, header required)

**Deduplication**: jobs are matched by `externalId`. If a match is found
and the content differs, the record is updated (`updated` counter). If
identical, it is skipped (`skipped` counter).

**Priority**: batch-ingested jobs rank below direct/vendor/api posts in
search results (`sourceType` field controls ordering).
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
(Required) CSV file (UTF-8, header row required)
Human-readable batch label

response.name: Async result (batch >100 jobs, queued for processing)

Returns processing progress for an async batch job.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Job application submission and pipeline management

Submits a job application. Designed for a ≤60-second mobile flow.

**Unauthenticated** seekers must supply `name` and `phone`.
**Authenticated** seekers have their profile snapshot applied automatically;
`name` and `phone` can be omitted.

Resume upload is always optional. If the seeker has a stored default resume
it is attached automatically (authenticated only) unless overridden.

If the job has `quickHire = true` and interview slots are available,
`interviewSlotId` should be provided to book a slot atomically.

Rate limited: 10 applications per seeker per hour.

Moves an applicant through the pipeline stages.
Only the owning employer or an admin may call this endpoint.

Valid transitions:
- `new → reviewing → shortlisted → interview → offer → hired`
- `any → rejected` or `any → withdrawn` (terminal states)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

RESTful API for the WarehouseJobs.com platform.

## Authentication
Most endpoints require a `Bearer` JWT issued by the WarehouseJobs auth service
(`POST /api/v1/auth/login`). Pass it in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

Three roles are recognised:
- **admin** – platform operators; full access to all resources
- **vendor** – authenticated employers; manage their company's jobs and applicants
- **seeker** – job seekers; apply to jobs, view their own applications

Batch ingestion endpoints additionally accept a long-lived `X-Api-Key` header
issued to trusted scrapers and feed partners.


Contact Support:
 Name: WarehouseJobs Engineering
 Email: dev@warehousejobs.com
